text: Republic of the Congo: Congolese army leader Mobutu appears to have strengthened his position as a result of his 16 October talks in Elisabethville with President Tshombé of separatist Katanga Province. Tshombé's endorsement of Mobutu's program for "neutralization" of Congolese political forces until 1 January--and in terms which implied that Katanga continues to regard itself as part of the Congo--may help to undercut the argument that only Lumumba can hold the Congo together. Meanwhile, Lumumba's principal African allies, evidently despairing of securing in Leopoldville a clear-cut decision favorable to Lumumba, now appear to be concentrating their efforts on achieving their ends through the UN, where they are pressing for the establishment and early dispatch to the Congo of a "good offices" committee. The UAR is a prime mover behind this new approach, and that Nasir's ambassador in Leopoldville has been counseling Lumumba to avoid "insecure adventures" while the "Afro-Asian neutralist countries mobilize world public opinion" on behalf of a "democratic solution
